claim: Roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.

result.claim: Roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.
result.reasoning:
While the search results confirm that variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (vCJD) prions can be found in lymphoid tissues and that prevalence studies have been conducted in the UK, no direct evidence was found to support the specific claim that "roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues."

Several propositions and chunks indicate that vCJD is associated with prion colonization of lymphoreticular tissues (prop_181711, Paper ID: 18849289) and that abnormal prion protein has been detected in lymphoid tissues of vCJD patients, including preclinically (Chunk ID: chunk_4907, Paper ID: W2008552288; Chunk ID: chunk_4884, Paper ID: 18849289).

Furthermore, the existence of "recent human prevalence studies" and "significant subclinical prion infection of the UK population" is mentioned (Chunk ID: chunk_4797, Paper ID: 24445428). It is also noted that the Chief Medical Officer announced an undertaking to estimate the prevalence of asymptomatic infection with vCJD prions (prop_180974, Paper ID: 18849289; Chunk ID: chunk_4809, Paper ID: 18849289).

However, despite these indications of ongoing research and the presence of prions in lymphoid tissues, none of the retrieved information provides the specific statistic of "one in twenty" or "5%" of the UK population harboring these prions. The evidence suggests that such prevalence studies exist, but the exact numerical claim is not directly supported by the available data.

evidence.text: An asymptomatic red cell transfusion recipient has been identified with prion protein deposition in a lymph node.
evidence.source: Prion infectivity in the spleen of a PRNP heterozygous individual with subclinical variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease

evidence.text: CJD transmission in western societies has occurred by accidental inoculation with human prions.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Variant CJD is caused by infection with BSE-like prions.
evidence.source: Molecular neurology of prion disease

evidence.text: In some rodent scrapie models, peripheral exposure is followed by prion replication to high levels in lymphoreticular tissues.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Kuru is a human prion disease
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Iatrogenic CJD associated with peripheral inoculation with human prions typically presents with ataxia.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Transfer of prion from the periphery to the brain in susceptible mice appeared to depend on the presence of circulating B lymphocytes.
evidence.source: Leucocyte Depletion Of The Blood Supply – How Will Patients Benefit?

evidence.text: Kuru is the principal human epidemic prion disease.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease (CJD) is a human prion disease.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Kuru is the only known major epidemic of human prion disease.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Four patients have been identified with vCJD prion infection after receiving blood transfusions from donors who subsequently developed vCJD.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: The patient with kuru did not have evidence of lymphoreticular colonization with prions in a tonsil biopsy.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: An asymptomatic red cell transfusion recipient, who did not die of variant Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease, has been identified with prion protein deposition in the spleen.
evidence.source: Prion infectivity in the spleen of a PRNP heterozygous individual with subclinical variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease

evidence.text: Kuru is a human epidemic prion disease.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Prion disease in mice has a highly distinctive and repeatable incubation time for a given prion strain in a defined inbred mouse line.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Variant Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease (vCJD) is thought to be transmitted by dietary prion exposure.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: 27 of the 372 patients (7%) carried likely pathogenic/pathogenic variants in the candidate genes.
evidence.source: Germline variants observed in pediatric cancer patients related to hereditary breast and ovarian cancer in adults.

evidence.text: The Chief Medical Officer announced an undertaking to estimate the prevalence of asymptomatic infection with vCJD prions.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: A patient diagnosed with vCJD had minimal deposition of disease-associated prion protein in peripheral lymphoreticular tissue.
evidence.source: Variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease with extremely low lymphoreticular deposition of prion protein.

evidence.text: Cases of iatrogenic infection with vCJD prions arising from blood transfusion have emerged.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: Cells of the innate immune system have been proven to be critical players in the initial pathogenesis of prion disease.
evidence.source: Prion Disease and the Innate Immune System

evidence.text: Lymphoreticular colonization with prions was not observed in the patient with kuru at tonsil biopsy.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.

evidence.text: vCJD is uniformly associated with high levels of prion colonization of the lymphoreticular tissues.
evidence.source: A clinical study of kuru patients with long incubation periods at the end of the epidemic in Papua New Guinea.
